text:
You do not choose a topic. You choose a patient.

Tom Hayes is in the resuscitation bay. Sarah Mensah is three days post-op and her numbers are moving in the wrong direction. An elderly man comes in with a sore throat that is not a sore throat.

You walk in. You work the case. Emergency, ICU, ward. Timed cases that move whether you are ready or not.

And the library does not run out. Because that is the other thing about reps. You need volume. You need to see cardiomyopathy presenting as crackles enough times that your hands know before your mind catches up. You need to see the sore throat that is an airway closing. You need to have been in that room before the day it matters.

This is that room. Before the day it matters.
